root_motivation: Automated /build ships binaries but the adoption step (cargo install → PATH) never runs and nothing verifies it. Tools sit uninstalled for days. The root gap is structural: binstale can only check a running process, so an artifact that was never installed is invisible to all existing detectors. adopt scan fills that gap with a read-only probe that answers, per shipped artifact, whether it was actually adopted and if not, what exact command to run.
user_persona: Joe Yen on the wintermute laptop, running daily self-review and /build automation. Needs a machine-readable verdict + copy-pasteable fix per artifact so downstream tools (adopt-apply, docket-report) can consume it.

acceptance_criteria.description: `adopt scan --format json` emits a JSON array; each element has at minimum `repo`, `bin`, `verdict`, `is_daemon`, and `fix_cmd` keys.
A wintermute repo declaring a [[bin]] whose binary is absent from PATH/~/.local/bin/~/.cargo/bin gets verdict `not-installed` and fix_cmd `cargo install --path <repo> --root ~/.local`.
A binary present on PATH whose installed mtime is newer than the repo's newest src/ commit gets verdict `installed-current`.
A binary present on PATH whose installed mtime is older than the repo's newest src/ commit gets verdict `installed-stale`.
A library-only repo (no [[bin]], no default-run) is reported `not-a-bin` and excluded from default output (shown only with --all).
An unreadable manifest, malformed Cargo.toml, or missing repo dir is skipped without aborting (exit 0, other artifacts still reported).
`adopt scan --format json | head -1` does not panic with SIGPIPE/BrokenPipe.
`adopt scan --match '^wm-'` restricts output to artifacts whose bin name starts with `wm-`.
When artifact is backed by a systemd user unit, is_daemon: true; when binstale absent, scan completes without error.

scope: Additive scan of shipped slugs from build manifest
Per-artifact verdict: not-installed, installed-stale, installed-current, not-a-bin
Produce copy-pasteable fix_cmd for each actionable verdict
SIGPIPE reset (sigpipe::reset() first line of main)
Daemon detection via systemd unit ExecStart lookup
mtime comparison with provfs xattr fallback
non_goals: No mutation: adopt scan is read-only, does not install anything
No daemon/process management (that is adopt-apply's job)
No tracking of running daemon staleness (that is binstale's job)
Does not build binaries from source
Does not manage PATH or shell config

additional.sigpipe_reset: sigpipe::reset() must be the first statement in main()

five_whys_trace.q: Why does /build ship binaries that are never used?
five_whys_trace.a: Because build = compile + commit + push, but adoption = cargo install to PATH is a separate manual step that has no enforcer.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does no existing tool catch this?
five_whys_trace.a: binstale operates on running process PIDs — it can't flag a binary that was never installed and therefore never runs. vigil/binstale cover stale daemons; nothing covers never-installed CLIs.
Self-review has flagged it in prose on six consecutive days, but prose notes have no machine-readable verdict and no actionable fix command wired to automation.

rollout — the tool whose job is adopting fleet binaries — is itself not installed (rollout absent from PATH for 9 days), meaning the fix for fleet staleness is itself the most obvious example of the problem.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just manually run cargo install now?
five_whys_trace.a: The problem is structural: there are many wintermute repos and no single command shows which need adoption. The detector must exist before the fixer can be wired into automation.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should adopt scan check live running processes for staleness?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. Running-process staleness stays binstale's job. adopt annotates is_daemon and can note binstale's verdict as context, but does not re-derive it.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the install root convention on this laptop?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: ~/.local/bin (cargo install --root ~/.local). ~/.cargo/bin is also checked but not the primary fix target.
